item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't much care for it. It did world building for a group I don't find interesting, and then, almost as an afterthought, had some warmed-over body horror. Well done, but not for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the letters. Nice showcase of someone descending into occultism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Parasites carrying curses" thing could be better if there simply was more parasites and more curses

Coming back to this after so long, since I have a proper response to this.

Yes indeed, when I drafted this, I thought of many different types of curses. Now, it is my stance that the rebirth system (and controlling females) are the only curses left.

I suppose that when Vaski hid among the Indians, he decided to prioritise certain abilities over others. By survival of the fittest and coupled with attacks by the original Thuggee, the more overt curses were rooted out over time and Vaski focused on 'reproduce more than we can be wiped out'.

And by the Victorian era, SCP-2833 is optimised to get mates and reproduce – a very sad degradation from their initial state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You don't need to describe each single parasite and what it does, in my opinion it would be enough with just a quick list of things like centipedes, silverfish and cholera. And the footnote sounds like a good idea. I don't even think that you need to mention his rank, just write (or add to the existing footnote) something like "According to information retrieved from the Global Occult Coolitation under Section ██ of the Sitra Archa Joint Agreement Sir William Henry Sleeman was known to have connections with a Neo-Sarkic organisation, possibly GoI-0490: Adytum's Wake or GoI-0385: Esoteric Order of White Worm"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that the "Parasites carrying curses" thing could be better if there simply was more parasites and more curses

Originally, I did intend for a wider variety of creatures to be spawned. I rescinded on that during drafting since I thought it focused too much on showing different types of creepy crawlies with different effects.

Why can't the parasites just carry actual pathogens, the "creepy bug making you hallucinate" thing has been used kind if much.

You may be on to something. The hallucinations can be a side-effect to other pathogens/chemicals generated by 2833-As… I will look back at it when I do an eventual update of this article.

Also, when I reread the letters it is apparent that Sleeman in fact has sarkic connections. I'm sorry, I appear to have missed that.

I am considering to note a footnote to indicate Sleeman's rank among his Sarkic Cult. Perhaps a low-ranking member will do for him, since I doubt he has ties to Hungarian-Romanian nobility that form the backbone of Neo-Sarkic leadership. Will get that down once I reconcile the 2nd point addressed here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that the "Parasites carrying curses" thing could be better if there simply was more parasites and more curses, and the actual "fly getting you pregnant" thing would be the exception rather than the norm, maybe something that they do only after death or when heavily incapitated. Why can't the parasites just carry actual pathogens, the "creepy bug making you hallucinate" thing has been used kind if much.
Also, when I reread the letters it is apparent that Sleeman in fact has sarkic connections. I'm sorry, I appear to have missed that.

Changed to neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't really find the SCP's decreasing mental state appearant

I hope you mean "apparent"… I don't think mental decline should be obvious. The insane ramblings are mixed with the experiences of multiple lives weighed down upon, the expectations of Karcist Vaski and perhaps the slightest bit of individualism (that is practically negligible).

SCP-2833-A instances sounded like something inserted into a B-movie for body horror's sake.

Granted, 2833-A was my take on Meta's "internalised reality bending curses".1 I took that idea and mixed it with a sub-plot from a relatively old Hong Kong show involving worms used as curses.

I can't really see the link between Sleeman's letters about the Thuggees and the actual SCP. Was he in some kind of lieu with the Sarkicites, and why? And how does he know about Adytum?

Sleeman would be associated with Neo-Sarkicites. As for why, I would note that Neo-Sarkicism is largely more cosmopolitan in nature, and I imagine it has plans to reach out to beyond the remoteness of their primitive counterparts. For Neo-Sarkicists to seek out members in the West and in the colonial administrators (who has further reach into regions that are potential fertile grounds), it feels natural. Going along those lines, a Sarkicist ought to know Adytum. It's like asking if a Christian knows Heaven or Hell.

As for the link that you cannot seem to notice, the Thuggees had opposed SCP-2833 over the years and largely kept them from swarming all over India. Sleeman, in an act of good faith to Proto-Sarkicists, launched persecutions and smear campaigns on the Thuggees to eliminate this threat.

Footnotes
1. Which did not last all the way till the unveiling of the Sarkcism Hub (to my slight shame).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I found this not doing anything for me. I didn't really find the SCP's decreasing mental state appearant, and the SCP-2833-A instances sounded like something inserted into a B-movie for body horror's sake. Also, I can't really see the link between Sleeman's letters about the Thuggees and the actual SCP. Was he in some kind of lieu with the Sarkicites, and why? And how does he know about Adytum?

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the original picture is in the Sarkic Hub sandbox (Proto-Sarkicism –> Vatula).

Reverse image search got me a few sites, but I'm not sure of their copyright status.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What was the original picture? Got at least a reverse image search?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…Because I uploaded the original picture earlier on and edited it a bit to suit the black-and-white images Sarkic SCPs tend to have.

It is more of an out-of-universe thing, on part of the author (i.e. me).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why are there two images of the guy in the files? One's colored and the other isn't?

I'd be weird of there was some image code where the images would change color or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirding

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stories tend to be exaggerated over time. Survivors tend to give wild incredible accounts of traumatic events.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah yes, it's pretty obvious in hindsight. The way I pictured this part of the story in my head, the final attack of the Colossi was a dramatic scenario, something like a great sacrifice that destroyed both sides at best, as opposed to a decisive victory which the word "overrun" implies. Blame me for watching too much mecha anime.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking that the flies could escape and continue the reproductive cycle even if the originator died.

Metaphysician's original concept for the Vatula was that they curse their enemies via reality bending organic matter, which I added my interpretation on how that's done – the flies are more akin to embodied curses (particularly body control and hallucination) and their reproductive function is more likely to be something utilised later on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, did a quick reread, just realized that it is the originating Vatula, not the mini Vatula, that controls people infected. I was thinking that the flies could escape and continue the reproductive cycle even if the originator died.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
strangulation doesn't seem all that foolproof a way to prevent them from spreading

You don't honestly think that the Children of Kali won't have their methods of protection against Vatula's bugs. In any case, if such protection exist, they are lost in the modern era. The Foundation and GOC would just isolate 2833 and roast them.

Maybe restrict it to just open wounds?

I did have it that way initially. Changed that due to a previous reviewer suggesting that there is no reason for it to be open wounds only… While there is one in the end, myths can't be accurate all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only real weakness I can see is that strangulation doesn't seem all that foolproof a way to prevent them from spreading, which slightly of undermines the Thugee connection. Where in the body the insect is generated and how long it takes aren't specified; one could generate the bug in the back of their throat and spit it out for example. Maybe restrict it to just open wounds? Would strengthen the Raktabija connection.

Edit: did a quick reread, cleared up most of my questions.

The above is just fine tuning. Liked it overall. Am inordinately proud of myself for twigging to the Sarkic connection early on.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I may add on, 2833's reproduction is an allusion to the demon Raktabija (which can grow a copy of itself should blood be spilt). Since the "mythological" roots of the Thuggee date back to Kali's battle with Raktabija, I would perceive an ancient battle between the Vatula (2833) and Kali's followers as an inspiration for the myth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed on that, but I can't figure out if the old image was CC/Public Domain… so I opted for a law-abiding option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One thing I really like about this is the explanation for the Thuggee strangulations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, the other one reminded me more of the Karcist images you have used. This guy just looks like a Shaivite, and therefore totally normal. I don't see anything creepy about it all (besides the creepiness I feel about theism in general, but statically speaking I am an outlier there).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I rather like this one. The other one was too… artistic? The flaws of this image adds more realism for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's gone, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the creepy-ass picture. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am sad that the new image isn't nearly as cohesive with the other Karcist images as the old one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't find this too interesting, though there's that word "Karcist" again. Description could use some cleanup, there are a lot of really convoluted phrases ("While initially believed to be an unique entity, it was disproved when four instances").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Males lack the biological organ to keep the preformed 2833. Furthermore, I would imagine that 2833 still retains the social concept that women serve as child-bearers, not men (who would be the warriors and priests).

With all 2833 instances as males, they enforce an "all-male" group and outsource females a la the inverse Amazons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I receive far more of a helminthiasis vibe from these guys— a free-roaming phase, a larval phase (SCP-2833-A), and a parasitic, reproductive phase. If anything, that particular life cycle interests me more than any Sarkicist history.

But given its capapbilities, why reproduce only in women?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every reference to the Colossi. I wrote them as the ones who invaded Adytum, which led to Vaski fleeing to India (I would picture the Colossi's presence to be an apocalyptic event for some Sarkicists).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like sarkcist SCPs in general but… somehow I am not feeling this one. The crosslink to 029 doesn't help because while it fits, I don't like 029 as it's just another old Foundation-style mary sue what kills you. Also as other posters said already, we had this type of body horror in many other skips.

I'm not downvoting but no upvote either.

Which parts of the article are referencing 2406? I think I may have missed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm always chuffed to see Indian SCPs on the wiki, and even though I've seen dozens of Shaivite and Vaishnavite sages, the overexposure on that photo makes it supremely creepy. That crosslink to SCP-029 is also damn good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't understand this sentence.

GOC were attacking the gang of SCP-2833, and Foundation agents stepped in after it caused some ruckus. That's the chronology of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, although for some reason the body horror didn't have the same impact as in the draft I read. More than made up for by the density of the rest of it - I particularly like how neatly 029 was woven in.

A few drafting points:

These SCP-2833 instances were previously ambushed by GOC operatives, wherein an SCP-2833 instance was terminated as a result.

I didn't understand this sentence. Were the 2833 instances ambushed before the Foundation found them (and how did the Foundation know either that it was the GOC or that one of the instances died)? And "wherein" doesn't seem correct - "whereby" is closer (although wouldn't require "as a result" at the end of the sentence), but I think "resulting in the death of an SCP-2833 instance" is probably better.

  • "overran" should be "overrun"
  • "the "Daughter of Shadow" survive the implantation ritual." should be "survived".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does not. You're free to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, well, they wrote it all. I just presented them with the basic idea - does that count?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember reviewing this. Liked it then, liked it now.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is this a collaboration between you and Metaphysician? If so, Meta can not upvote. If this isn't the case, disregard this. I'm merely asking due to the second paragraph in your post. It's not too clear.

This is certainly interesting with worldbuilding, but the specific type of body horror you go for here is very worn out for me. I can't even be fazed because there's at least three different skips that go for this kind of body horror you go for here on the mainlist, and I just can't care. Maybe I'm tired of body horror in general as well; for it to really grab me in text form, it either has to be vividly described or, barring that (since that kind of thing is near-impossible in a clinical document), go all-out in its effects and imagery to boot (visuals or textual).

The journal at the end was particularly wearisome to go through. It help set up the history of the anomaly and did some more worldbuilding for the Sarkicists, but it wasn't entertaining and it was hard to keep my attention for it.

This is otherwise well-written and executed (even if it's not my thing), and the interviews were good to help make this more interesting. The image is also pretty good, though you should see if it falls under the CC Policy. I just can't get into this like the other articles of its kind, and it didn't elicit a reaction from me other than wanting to get through it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed it! You have my stamp of approval :)

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
This is my attempt at a Sarkicist SCP. I would like to thank all the people who had reviewed it, like psul and Amuness etc. Especially, I wish to thank Metaphysician for his openness to this concept which I have suggested.

Metaphysician pretty much did the skeleton of it (basic concept of the Vatula), although I'm the one who added the flesh to the piece and made it an SCP.

Attribution-wise, this article has the following references:

  • SCP-2406 ("The Colossus") by Metaphysician
  • SCP-029 ("Daughter of Shadows") by TheDuckman
  • SCP-140 (An Incomplete Chronicle) by AssertiveRoland
  • Wrong Proposal (The Consensus) by MrWrong

Image is attributed to Archit Ratan of Flickr (https://www.flickr.com/photos/architratan/7378338908) under CC BY 2.0.

And a few more things…

+ show block
– hide block

The Vatula/Karcist Vaski are basically reincarnating themselves since Karcist Vaski went to India. With each generation (cycle of reincarnation), less of their sanity is retained over time. By the time the Foundation discovers them, most SCP-2833 instances barely remember who they are and it is reasonable to say they're lunatics at this point.
